

```{r}

# dependencies
library(metafor)
library(psychmeta)

# simulated data
dat <- data.frame(
  study_id = c("S1", "S2"),
  m_trt    = c(3.8, 4.1),
  sd_trt   = c(0.9, 0.8),
  n_trt    = c(50, 40),
  m_ctrl   = c(3.2, 3.3),
  sd_ctrl  = c(1.0, 0.9),
  n_ctrl   = c(48, 42),
  rel_y    = c(0.85, 0.80),   # reliability of the outcome (e.g., alpha)
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# calculate ES
dat_es <- escalc(
  measure = "SMD",
  m1i = m_trt, sd1i = sd_trt, n1i = n_trt,
  m2i = m_ctrl, sd2i = sd_ctrl, n2i = n_ctrl,
  data = dat,
  var.names = c("d", "v_d")  # rename yi, vi -> d, v_d
)

# meta
ma_psychometric <- ma_d(
  d          = d,
  n1         = n_trt,
  n2         = n_ctrl,
  sample_id  = study_id,
  ma_method  = "ic",          # probably needs thought
  ryy        = rel_y,
  ryy_type   = "alpha",       # probably needs thought
  correct_bias = FALSE,       # already applied by escalc
  data       = dat_es
)

# results
ma_psychometric$meta_tables$`analysis_id: 1`$individual_correction |>
  select(mean_delta, # latent, not observed
         CI_LL_95, CI_UL_95)

```
